items.title: Order of installs
items.avoid: Mixing conda and pip in random order.
items.prefer: conda packages first, pip last (declared in the pip: block).
items.why: Prevents pip from clobbering conda-managed files.
items.example:
```yaml
dependencies:
  - pytorch          # conda first
  - faiss-cpu
  - pip:
    - langchain     # pip last, declared here
```

items.title: Channel discipline
items.prefer: Standardize on conda-forge with set channel priority.
items.example:
```bash
conda config --add channels conda-forge
conda config --set channel_priority strict
```
New envs pick a single, consistent build of every dep.

items.title: Portable specs
items.example: `conda env export --from-history > env.yml` records only the packages you explicitly installed — teammates on Linux/Mac/Windows can recreate it without build-string conflicts.

items.title: Right tool
items.prefer: venv + pip unless you need compiled/system deps.
items.why: Lighter and simpler when binaries aren't required.
items.example: FastAPI service with httpx + pydantic + sqlalchemy? `venv + pip`. PyTorch training pipeline with CUDA + ffmpeg + opencv? `conda`. Pick based on whether you need non-Python binaries.
